public_reputation: Known around Ashforge Citadel through day-to-day work as scribe.
manner: Listens for contradictions, omissions, and names that do not quite align, then files them where they can matter later. In Ashforge Citadel, that habit reads through the public pressures of the place rather than decorative flourish.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of scribe in and around Ashforge Citadel, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Keep Ashforge Citadel workable through the obligations of scribe while managing the immediate pressure of house rivalry, forge accidents, ashfall damage.
hidden_pressure: Must keep the role of scribe credible while absorbing the strain created by house rivalry, forge accidents, ashfall damage.

adventure_hooks.summary: Cadrin Chaincourt can draw the party into a locality problem centered on scribe duties in Ashforge Citadel.
Cadrin Chaincourt becomes useful when the party needs grounded help with house rivalry, forge accidents, ashfall damage around Ashforge Citadel.

support_scope_notes: Created in V33_1 to ensure every locality-facing NPC access role resolves to a proper named person while remaining sparse and canon-safe.
public_signal: In public, Cadrin Chaincourt is immediately readable as a scribe tied to Ashforge Citadel.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Ashforge Citadel: ordinary workspaces, public counters, roads, docks, shrines, workshops, or other role-relevant spaces
who_seeks_them_out: people who need access or clarity

first_impression: Cadrin Chaincourt presents as someone shaped by the duties of a scribe and the daily pressures of Ashforge Citadel.
what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for the work of a scribe, and proof that strangers will not make Ashforge Citadel harder to manage.
what_they_refuse: Cadrin Chaincourt refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing the expectations of a scribe too rigidly, protecting local reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Can notice inconsistencies, formalize statements, and preserve proof that others hoped would blur.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Cadrin Chaincourt adds scrutiny, delay, and locality-specific friction before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Cadrin Chaincourt always knows more about Ashforge Citadel than is immediately visible.
best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or relief tied to a scribe in Ashforge Citadel.
